skill.name: security-audit
skill.description: Guides structured security reviews, OWASP assessments, vulnerability reporting, and remediation planning for software teams.
skill.summary: Create OWASP-aligned security audit reports with findings, evidence, severity, and remediation steps.
skill.author: Joseph OBrien

security_audit.summary: Static findings were reviewed in context and are false positives from markdown placeholders, checklist terms, and fenced report examples. No executable scripts, network calls, credential access, prompt injection, or malicious intent were found. The skill has low dual-use risk because it helps document security findings and proof-of-concept evidence.

low_findings.title: Dual-Use Security Reporting Template
low_findings.description: The report template includes fields for proof-of-concept details, remediation code, and scan outputs. This is appropriate for a security audit skill, but users should handle exploit evidence responsibly.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are markdown template sections for audit evidence, not executable code. The content is dual-use but does not provide exploit logic or automate attacks.

low_findings.title: Static Findings Dismissed as Non-Executable Markdown
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography, Windows SAM, prompt injection, and shell execution detections are false positives. They match ordinary security terminology, placeholder text, inline backticks, and fenced markdown blocks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows the flagged content is documentation and checklist material. No command execution syntax, cryptographic implementation, SAM database access, or prompt override instruction is present.

content.value_statement: Security reviews often miss structured evidence and remediation priorities. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through OWASP-aligned audits with clear findings.
actual_capabilities: Guides security code reviews with severity, CVSS, CWE, impact, and remediation fields.
Provides an OWASP Top 10 checklist for web application assessment.
Structures dependency, authentication, authorization, data protection, and infrastructure review sections.
Helps create remediation roadmaps grouped by urgency.
Formats audit evidence, scan outputs, and quality checks in a report template.
limitations: Does not execute scanners or validate vulnerabilities without user-provided evidence.
Cannot replace a licensed penetration test or formal compliance assessment.
Requires accurate scope, architecture, logs, and code context from the user.
May produce dual-use vulnerability details that require responsible handling.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Release Security Review
use_cases.description: Review application changes before launch and organize findings by severity, impact, and remediation priority.

use_cases.title: Create an OWASP Assessment
use_cases.description: Map application controls and findings to OWASP Top 10 categories with checklist coverage.

use_cases.title: Plan Vulnerability Remediation
use_cases.description: Convert audit evidence into short-term and medium-term remediation tasks for product teams.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Single Finding
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of one vulnerability report item.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this security finding. Confirm severity, impact, CWE mapping, and remediation clarity. Identify missing evidence or unclear assumptions.

prompt_templates.title: Run an OWASP Checklist Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Structured assessment against common web application risks.
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess this application scope against the OWASP Top 10 checklist. Report tested areas, likely gaps, evidence needed, and prioritized next steps.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Security Audit Report
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a complete report from supplied review notes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the security audit format to draft a report from these notes. Include executive summary, findings, impact, recommendations, and remediation roadmap.

prompt_templates.title: Prioritize Remediation Across Teams
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning for multiple findings and owners.
prompt_templates.prompt: Prioritize these security findings by exploitability, business impact, dependencies, and effort. Create a phased remediation plan with ownership assumptions.

output_examples.input: Review notes for an authentication issue with missing account lockout.
output_examples.output: A finding summary with medium severity, CWE mapping, impact, recommended lockout controls, and evidence gaps.

output_examples.input: A dependency scan summary with several vulnerable packages.
output_examples.output: A dependency audit section listing affected packages, severity, fix priority, and upgrade guidance.

output_examples.input: A release scope covering APIs, authentication, and cloud configuration.
output_examples.output: An audit outline with in-scope components, OWASP coverage, key risks, and a remediation roadmap.

best_practices: Provide clear scope, system context, and evidence before asking for risk ratings.
Verify every finding with reproducible evidence before sharing it outside the team.
Review generated remediation steps with the system owner before implementation.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill to target systems without authorization.
Do not treat severity ratings as final without validating business impact.
Do not publish proof-of-concept details before coordinating responsible disclosure.

faq.question: Can this skill run security scanners?
faq.answer: No. It organizes audit work and reports based on evidence that the user provides.

faq.question: Does it support OWASP Top 10 reviews?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a checklist for OWASP Top 10 categories and related control checks.

faq.question: Can it assign CVSS scores?
faq.answer: It can help draft CVSS-based severity assessments, but users must validate each score.

faq.question: Is this suitable for compliance certification?
faq.answer: No. It can support preparation, but it is not a formal audit authority.

faq.question: What evidence should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide code snippets, affected components, logs, scanner output, architecture notes, and business context.

faq.question: Can it help with remediation planning?
faq.answer: Yes. It can group fixes by urgency, impact, and implementation sequence.
